hypothesis: State-conditioned arithmetic relations reduce normalized witness growth below the Pell quotient.
limitations: does not prove actual witness-complexity divergence
does not prove the alternating inverse lift has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The three conditional identities, terminating reduction, six-state automaton, characteristic polynomial, growth bound, and almost-sure rate are all-width statements proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_canonical_relations.md. Exhaustive campaigns are bounded regression checks.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.canonical_representatives: every normalized arithmetic witness has a same-length representative accepted by the six-state automaton
exact_conclusions.growth_bound: phase arithmetic-image growth is O(lambda^N), lambda largest root of lambda^3-2lambda^2-lambda+1
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: prove divergence for the one deterministic zero-initialized fringe schedule
